refactoring_goal: Separate web UI files from Rust source code by moving frontend assets to web-ui/ directory while keeping npm commands executable from project root

package_json.reason: User wants npm commands from root

package_lock.reason: User wants npm commands from root

vite_config.reason: User wants npm commands from root, but needs path updates

legacy_js.reason: Old Web Components, replaced by Lit components

legacy_html.reason: Old HTML entry point, replaced by root index.html

vite_config.build_output: Update to '../dist' to keep dist in project root

tasks_completed: Identify web UI files vs Rust files
Map file movements and path updates

validation_checklist: Development mode: cd web-ui && npm run dev
Production build: cd web-ui && npm run build
notes: This restructure separates concerns clearly: Rust in /src/, Web UI in /web-ui/
Build output remains in /dist/ so Rust server needs no changes
Legacy files in /static/js/ are unused since Lit migration
New structure matches modern full-stack project conventions
After refactor: development requires cd web-ui before npm commands
